英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾
zh.Croft, Leicestershire, England: 英國萊希斯特斯爾卡芙特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Robert Clements of Croft於1481年出生在英國萊希斯特斯爾卡芙特時，他的父親Sir William Clements42歲，母親Lady Anne Bailey Barley32歲。他於從1504年1月11日到1505年1月10日與Sybil Isabel Llwyd在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1558年在他的家鄉享年77歲。

英国英格兰萊希斯特斯爾
zh-hans.Croft, Leicestershire, England: 英国萊希斯特斯爾卡芙特
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William Robert Clements of Croft于1481年出生在英国萊希斯特斯爾卡芙特时，他的父亲Sir William Clements42岁，母亲Lady Anne Bailey Barley32岁。他于从1504年1月11日到1505年1月10日与Sybil Isabel Llwyd在英国英格兰萊希斯特斯爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1558年在他的家乡享年77岁。

nameMeanings.name: Clements
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Irish (Tyrone and Antrim), and Dutch: variant of Clement , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. German Clementz and Slovenian patronymic Klemenc (see Klements ).

variants.name: Clemence
meanings: English and French: from the Middle English and Old French female personal name Clemence (from Latin clementia ‘mildness’).
English: variant of   Clements  .
In some cases also an Americanized form of Slovenian Klemenc (see Klements ).

variants.name: Clement
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Alcide, Alphonse, Andre, Marcel, Armand, Cecile, Christophe, Gaston, Olivier, Aime.

English, Catalan, German, Flemish, and Dutch; French and Walloon (Clément): from the Latin personal name Clemens meaning ‘merciful’ (genitive Clementis). This achieved popularity firstly through having been borne by an early Christian saint who was a disciple of Saint Paul, and later because it was selected as a symbolic name by a number of early popes. There has also been some confusion with the personal name Clemence (from Latin Clementia, meaning ‘mercy’, an abstract noun derived from the adjective; in part a masculine name from Latin Clementius, a later derivative of Clemens). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, especially Italian Clemente , and also their derivatives.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Croft
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of several places, for example in Lincolnshire, Lancashire, Herefordshire, and North Yorkshire, all named with Old English croft ‘small arable enclosure (normally adjoining a house)’, or a topographic name for someone who lived by a croft, from Middle English croft.
English: possibly in some cases a variant of   Craft   2.
Americanized form of German, Danish, Swedish, or Jewish   Kraft  . Compare   Kroft   and   Craft  .

variants.name: Craft
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English craft ‘skillful, clever, learned’.
English: habitational name from Croft in Leicestershire. See also   Croft  .
Americanized form of German, Danish, Swedish, or Jewish   Kraft  . See also   Croft  .

variants.name: Crate
meanings: English (Hampshire and Surrey): from Middle English crate ‘hurdle, basket’, or occasionally perhaps from crete ‘cradle’, probably applied as a metonymic occupational name for a maker of such articles. Alternatively, sometimes perhaps a variant of Croft 1.

variants.name: Crofts
meanings: English: variant of Croft , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Groft
meanings: English: perhaps an altered form of   Croft  .

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: equivalent of George .

variants.name: George
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Robert Clements of Croft was born in 1481, in Croft, Leicestershire, England, his father, Sir William Clements, was 42 and his mother, Lady Anne Bailey Barley, was 32. He married Sybil Isabel Llwyd from 11 January 1504 to 10 January 1505, in Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1558, in his hometown, at the age of 77.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William Robert Clements of Croft est né en 1481, à Croft, Leicestershire, Angleterre, son père, Sir William Clements, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Lady Anne Bailey Barley, avait 32 ans. Il a épousé Sybil Isabel Llwyd du 11 janvier 1504 au 10 janvier 1505, à Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1558, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 77 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William Robert Clements of Croft 1481 in Croft, Leicestershire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir William Clements 42 und seine Mutter Lady Anne Bailey Barley 32. Er hat von 11. Januar 1504 bis 10. Januar 1505 in Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom Sybil Isabel Llwyd geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1558 im Alter von 77 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Robert Clements of Croft è nato il nel 1481 nel Croft, Leicestershire, England, il suo padre, Sir William Clements, aveva 42 anni e il suo madre, Lady Anne Bailey Barley, aveva 32 anni. Lui sposato Sybil Isabel Llwyd da 11 gennaio 1504 a 10 gennaio 1505, nel Leicestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1558, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 77 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William Robert Clements of Croftが1481年にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアクロフトで生まれた時，父Sir William Clementsは42歳，母Lady Anne Bailey Barleyは32歳であった。から1504年1月11日まで1505年1月10日にイギリスイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアでSybil Isabel Llwydと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1558年に故郷で77歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Robert Clements of Croft(이)가 1481년 영국 레이스스터실르 크로프트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William Clements(은)는 42세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Anne Bailey Barley(은)는 32세였다. 그는 1504년 1월 11일부터 1505년 1월 10일까지 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르에서 Sybil Isabel Llwyd(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1558년 그의 고향에서 77세의 나이로 사망했다.

Он умер в 1558 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 77 лет.
